evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: building and scaling the data foundations that enable fast, reliable, and actionable insights
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD explicitly balances velocity ("fast", "move quickly") with rigour ("reliable", data quality checks), giving parity.
evidence.domain_risk: empower local economies shapes how our team members move quickly, learn, and reiterate
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Marketplace/logistics business decisions affecting Dashers, merchants, and consumers, but no financial reporting or regulatory compliance language present.
evidence.data_team_maturity: The Analytics Engineering team builds internal data products enabling data-driven decisions across business units.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A named, established Analytics Engineering team building canonical datasets and self-serve tools across business units indicates a mature, specialised data organisation.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design metrics and data visualizations with dashboarding tools like Tableau, Sigma, and Mode
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific dashboarding tools (Tableau, Sigma, Mode) and ETL/ELT pipeline work give a concrete picture of daily responsibilities.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: The Analytics Engineering team builds internal data products enabling data-driven decisions across business units.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centres on building internal data products and self-serve tools for data consumers across the company rather than a specific commercial or finance function.
evidence.autonomy_level: Lead the development of data products and self-serve tools
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: "Lead the development of" data products is direction-setting language for a key organisational capability.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool or AI infrastructure mention appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.testing_framing: Experience in writing data quality checks to validate data integrity
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Writing data quality checks is named as a hands-on practice the candidate performs, indicating owned responsibility rather than a passive tool mention.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Experience in writing data quality checks to validate data integrity
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality/integrity is a stated concern, but the JD frames this as pipeline reliability rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build and maintain canonical datasets by developing high-volume, reliable ETL/ELT pipelines
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes building new data products and canonical datasets with maintaining existing high-volume ETL/ELT pipelines.
evidence.loss_aversion: Experience in writing data quality checks to validate data integrity — reflects concern for data reliability rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
